title: Robert Langer | Koch Institute
text:
Section Title: Robert S. Langer > Research Areas > Research Summary
Content:
The Langer Laboratory works at the interface of biotechnology and materials science to address a wide range of problems in human health, including the detection, monitoring, and treatment of cancer. A major focus is the study and development of materials such as polymers and lipids to deliver drugs, particularly genetically engineered proteins and DNA and RNA, continuously at controlled rates for prolonged periods of time. In addition, we are developing drugs that specifically inhibit the process of neovascularization that is critical to several disease processes, including cancer, without interfering with existing blood vessels. We have also been involved in creating approaches to engineer new tissues. In particular, we are synthesizing new systems to be used in mammalian cell transplants to create liver, cartilage, pancreas, and nerves.

Section Title: Robert S. Langer > Research Areas > Biography
Content:
Robert Langer is one of nine Institute Professors at MIT; being an Institute Professor is the highest honor that can be awarded to a faculty member. He has written over 1,500 articles, which have been cited over 396,000 times; his h-index of 313 is the highest of any engineer in history and the 2nd highest of any individual in any field. His patents have licensed or sublicensed to over 400 companies; he is a cofounder of a number of companies including Moderna. Dr Langer served as Chairman of the FDA’s Science Board (its highest advisory board) from 1999-2002.

Section Title: ... > [The Magic of Engineering](https://pubs.acs.org/doi/10.1021/acsnanomed.5c00086)
Content:
ACS Nanomedicine
Robert Langer chats with *ACS Nanomedicine* about his “imperative to push medicine into new frontiers,” touching on moments when the translation of lab discoveries into technological innovations is accelerated. He also shares a few tricks of the trade for maintaining a successful lab—and reveals that he mayhavea few literal [tricks up his sleeve](https://www.drugdiscoveryonline.com/doc/video-spotlight-where-science-meets-magic-with-mit-institute-professor-robert-s-langer-0001) (see 21:50).
[](https://news.mit.edu/2025/more-precise-way-edit-genome-0917)
